mechanism_narrative: A sustained 5-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in the manufacturing sector that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 7.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in recession periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% signals a monetary easing cycle that reduces borrowing costs and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 1.2 million signals an increase in construction activity that boosts economic growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real GDP below 1.5% signals a weakening economy that reduces corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in recession periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in payroll employment above 150,000 signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate below 2.5% and a rise in the unemployment rate above 7.5% signals a monetary easing cycle that reduces borrowing costs and boosts consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real GDP above 2.5% and a decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a strengthening economy that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
